## About
- Name: Rahul NS Anand
- Summary: Rahul NS Anand. View Projects (/projects). Read Blogs (/blogs). Contact Rahul (/contact). Roles: Creator & BDFL - lyfie.org (2026 - Present). Founded Lyfie.org to build open-source products that make daily life and workflows simpler, faster, and better. Built and shipped a multi-product portfolio from zero: Papyra, Luthor, SantaClaws, and TickTrack. Owned product strategy, architecture, UI/UX, engineering, docs, and release execution end-to-end as creator and BDFL. Turned complex systems into simple products with fast iteration, strong DX, and real user impact.

  URL: https://www.rahulnsanand.com/blogs/designing-a-quiet-developer-portfolio
  Tags: Design Systems, Frontend Architecture, UX Writing
  Description: How I designed a minimal portfolio UI with stronger hierarchy, calmer motion, and better readability without losing personality.
  Excerpt: Why this design direction Most developer portfolios are loud by default. They have high-contrast gradients, too many animations, and five CTAs fighting for attention. That style can work, but it rarely survives long-term maintenance. I wanted this portfolio to feel intentional and premium while staying calm. The target interaction was simple: a recruiter or engineering manager

  Excerpt: Why this topic matters AI features fail in production for predictable reasons: ambiguous prompts, unvalidated outputs, hidden assumptions, and no fallback behavior. Most of these failures are not model problems. They are system design problems. Start with bounded outcomes When a feature accepts free-form user input and returns open-ended text, quality is unstable. I prefer task

  URL: https://www.rahulnsanand.com/blogs/from-side-project-to-credible-product-signal
  Tags: Career Growth, Product Engineering, Portfolio
  Description: How to position personal projects so they communicate architecture depth, product judgment, and execution quality to technical interviewers.
  Excerpt: The issue with most side projects Many projects show effort but not engineering maturity. They often demonstrate implementation, yet skip tradeoffs, constraints, and long-term thinking. Hiring teams look for signal, not just screenshots. Three things that improve signal quality Show constraints explicitly A project becomes credible when you describe real constraints: budget limits performance targets device
- Lessons From Reducing Frontend Bundle Noise in a Personal Site (2026-01-14)
  URL: https://www.rahulnsanand.com/blogs/lessons-from-reducing-frontend-bundle-noise
  Tags: Performance, Frontend, Web Vitals
  Description: A practical log of decisions that cut unnecessary client-side weight while preserving polish, responsiveness, and theme consistency.
  Excerpt: Performance work starts with scope discipline Bundle size optimization is often treated as a late-stage task. In practice, the biggest gains come from early scope decisions. If a page can render on the server, keep it server-first. What I measured first Before changing code, I listed high-risk sources of bloat: client-only wrappers around static sections icon
